commit.message:
fix: run_update blocked by stale firmware status cache

check_updates uses a two-condition check (fw_status=="update" OR
product_latest != product_version) to handle stale daemon cache.
run_update only checked fw_status=="none", causing it to block when
the cache returned stale "up to date" despite 26.1.3 being available.

Apply the same logic to run_update so both tools agree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
add jetlag-metadata Swift CLI wrapper for ExifTool (#125)

* add jetlag-metadata Swift CLI wrapper for ExifTool

Phase 1 of the swift-migration spec: introduce a Swift CLI tool
(jetlag-metadata) that sits between Python scripts and ExifTool,
establishing a JSON-over-stdin/stdout API contract. This decouples
the scripts from ExifTool's Perl interface so later phases can swap
in native Swift metadata handling without touching callers.

- New Swift package at macos/Sources/Tools/jetlag-metadata/ that
  wraps ExifTool's -stay_open mode behind a persistent JSON protocol
  supporting read and write operations
- New Python shim (scripts/lib/metadata.py) that tries the Swift
  backend first, falling back to the Python ExifTool wrapper
- All four scripts and conftest.py import from lib.metadata instead
  of lib.exiftool — the API surface is identical
- Build script (scripts/tools/build-jetlag-metadata.sh) and Xcode
  build phase updated to compile and bundle the CLI tool
- All 323 existing tests pass unchanged against the new backend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add dedicated tests for jetlag-metadata CLI

- 17 tests covering the JSON-over-stdin/stdout protocol: read operations
  (timestamps, camera tags, fast mode, missing tags, nonexistent files),
  write operations (camera, timestamps, nonexistent files), protocol
  robustness (unknown ops, invalid JSON, wrong tag types), and parity
  checks against direct ExifTool invocation
- Completes the Phase 1 test requirements from the swift-migration spec:
  JSON round-trip tests for each operation, and integration tests
  comparing output against ExifTool
- Full suite passes: 340 tests with Swift backend active

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Compare table: dynamic column headers for savings/TD + fee freshness

COMPARE TABLE DYNAMIC HEADERS:
  When the user selects 'Savings' or 'Term Deposits' filter,
  the desktop table column headers now adapt:

  Default (shares/crypto/CFD):
    ASX Fee | US Fee | FX Rate | CHESS | SMSF

  Savings / Term Deposits:
    Rate | Min Deposit | Conditions | ADI | Online

  The tooltips also change contextually:
  - 'Rate' tooltip: 'Annual interest rate (with conditions met)'
    for savings, 'Term deposit rate (6-month term)' for TDs
  - 'Min Deposit' replaces 'US Fee'
  - 'Conditions' replaces 'FX Rate'
  - 'ADI' replaces 'CHESS' (government deposit guarantee)
  - 'Online' replaces 'SMSF'

  Previously, savings accounts showing '5.50% p.a.' under an
  'ASX Fee' header was confusing. Now the header matches the data.

DATABASE:
  Set fee_last_checked = NOW() on all 73 active platforms.
  Previously the 40 new platforms had null, meaning the
  'Fees verified X days ago' indicator showed nothing on their
  review pages. Now all platforms show the freshness indicator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(opencode): re-enable plan and build agents

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 5 Radiogenomics — Precision Oncology view in Patient Profile

Adds a "Precision Oncology" tab to the Patient Profile page that integrates
genomic variants, imaging outcomes, and treatment history into a unified
clinical decision support view. The RadiogenomicsTab component displays
variant-drug correlations, precision recommendations (drugs to avoid/consider),
variant table with ClinVar significance, and treatment history.

Validated with MBU patient: 17 variants, 7 correlations, 2 recommendations,
5 drug exposures, 13 imaging studies — KRAS G12D correctly identified as
resistant to anti-EGFR therapy and sensitive to bevacizumab/FOLFIRI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.23.0: Add SANDWORM_MODE supply chain, Copilot CLI CVE, multi-agent cascade attacks, semantic chaining, H-CoT hijacking, AI framework SSRF

ai-hunting:
- Add 7 new tools: Novee Security, Maze, Enkrypt AI MCP Scan, MCPScan.ai, MCPTox, Cortex AgentiX
- Add 15 MCP vulnerability classes: MCP SDK ReDoS (CVE-2026-0621), Pydantic-AI SSRF (CVE-2026-25904), Copilot CLI shell expansion (CVE-2026-29783), OpenClaw query string/TAR/SHA-1/pre-commit injection, GlicJack, ChainLeak, LangChain SSRF via redirect, SANDWORM_MODE, Agentgateway
- Add 8 real-world examples: SANDWORM_MODE, DockerDash, Copilot CLI malware download, PleaseFix/PerplexedBrowser, ForcedLeak, Operation Bizarre Bazaar, Notion AI, ChainLeak
- Add 4 new attack technique sections: semantic chaining jailbreak, H-CoT chain-of-thought hijacking, multi-agent privacy attacks (AgentLeak/OMNI-LEAK), SOUL.md identity file poisoning
- Add 12 MCP testing checklist items and 3 new prompt injection incidents

vuln-patterns:
- Add 12 new MCP test patterns: CLI allowlist bypass, query param credential relay, MCP SDK ReDoS, npm supply chain injection, Docker label injection, CRM form injection, multi-agent cascade, AI framework SSRF via redirect, semantic chaining, H-CoT, identity file poisoning
- Add 13 real-world references: SANDWORM_MODE, DockerDash, Copilot CLI, Enkrypt AI, MCPTox, CVE-2026-0621, CVE-2026-25904, ChainLeak, LangChain SSRF, Operation Bizarre Bazaar, ForcedLeak, PleaseFix/PerplexedBrowser

program-research:
- Add ~30 market context entries: Microsoft In Scope by Default, Apple $5M bounty, Novee $51.5M, Maze $31M, IBM X-Force exploitation stats, SANDWORM_MODE, Operation Bizarre Bazaar, ForcedLeak, ChainLeak, semantic chaining, H-CoT, AgentLeak, OMNI-LEAK
- Add 14 notable disclosed vulnerabilities and 7 competition awareness entries

hunt-session:
- Add 12 new rows to human-vs-AI edge matrix

report-review:
- Add 13 new AI/LLM checklist items: SANDWORM_MODE, meta-context injection, ForcedLeak, multi-agent cascade, semantic chaining, H-CoT, AI framework SSRF via redirect, identity file poisoning, ChainLeak, Pydantic-AI SSRF, OpenClaw query param relay
- Add 7 new edge cases for supply chain MCP injection, multi-agent cascades, won't-fix vendor responses, CRM injection, H-CoT, identity file poisoning
- Update competition awareness with Novee, Maze, Apple $5M, Enkrypt AI, MCPTox stats

commit.message:
Add pronunciation practice with retry and AI evaluation

- Retry button (🔄) on recorded user messages opens practice modal
- Modal shows the original text, Standard pronunciation (TTS), and
  all recording attempts with playback
- Users can record multiple attempts and compare with the standard
- AI Evaluate (🤖) sends recording to Gemini for bilingual
  pronunciation feedback (English + Chinese)
- Recordings persist within the chat session for comparison

commit.message:
Parallel Scryfall lookups with rate limiting and User-Agent header

- Scryfall lookups now run in parallel (ThreadPoolExecutor, 10 workers)
- Added _scryfall_get() helper with threading lock + 100ms delay between
  calls to respect Scryfall's ~10 req/sec rate limit
- Added required User-Agent and Accept headers to all Scryfall requests

Compact FORMAT_SPEC (-49%) and add act labels for stance clarity

FORMAT_SPEC: 167→85 words. Spec/raw ratio 151%→99% (overhead eliminated).
Act labels (#disagree, #prefer, etc.) appended to stance-critical codes
so LLMs understand who agreed/disagreed without parsing 2-char act codes.
V4 word savings 50.7%→69.7%, context extension 2.0x→3.3x, topics 6→7/8.

commit.message:
fix: dashboard login 'Failed to fetch' door HTTPS/HTTP mixed-content

- nginx.conf: proxy_pass alles wat geen statisch bestand is naar control_api:8080
  (try_files $uri @api → proxy_pass http://control_api:8080)
- login.html + index.html: API base URL van absolute URL naar relatief pad (leeg)
  Voorheen: fetch(`https://89.167.3.199:8080/auth/...`) → SSL fout
  Nu: fetch(`/auth/...`) → nginx proxyt intern naar control_api

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(strategy-miner): P0/P1 系统优化 — KB过滤+preflight+trade惩罚+market orders

P0 改动（修反馈回路 + 降成本）:
- P0-1: KB elite 过滤 — constraints_ok 检查阻止不合格候选进入精英库
- P0-2: 基础设施失败不浪费 iteration（_gen_retries 重试计数器）
- P0-3: backtest/repair 成功后清除 failure_category/diagnosis
- P0-4: repair clamp 从 max(3,...) 改为 max(1,...)
- P0-5: openai_compatible provider 纯代码输出（移除工具标签）
- P0-6: preflight freqtrade list-strategies 检查
- P0-7: max_parallel_roles 默认 2→1

P1 改动（提升策略质量）:
- P1-8: 市场档案注入到 prompt（_build_market_profile）
- P1-9: max_drawdown_pct 百分比约束
- P1-10: 交易数量惩罚 effective_sharpe = sharpe * min(1, trades/30)
- P1-11: 默认 market orders（避免 0-trade）
- P1-12: Analysis 结构化 JSON 输出

新增 11 个测试覆盖所有改动，79 tests passing。

chore: add compaction resilience and align with CC v2.1.63-69 (#69)

## Summary
- Add Task-based compaction resilience to 6 long-running skills and
full-review
- Normalize YAML frontmatter for Claude Code v2.1.63-69 compatibility
- Add release-please PR format configuration

## Changes
- **rules**: State Management section — task lifecycle, compact findings
format, recovery protocol, fix planning
- **skills + full-review**: State Management in 6 skills + full-review;
full-review 4→2+2 batch fix
- **agents + skills**: YAML frontmatter normalized to list format for CC
v2.1.63-69
- **docs**: Compaction Resilience sections in architecture.md and
skills.md
- **release-please-config.json**: PR title/body format configuration
